name: elder gods
summary: Distorted, hilarious beings whose cachinnating chorus livens the dream-sense of spectral whirling through liquid gulfs that followed the storm of April 2nd.
description:
In the dream-sense that follows the storm of April 2nd in Johansen's record — a spectral whirling through liquid gulfs of infinity, dizzying rides through reeling universes on a comet's tail, hysterical plunges from the pit to the moon and back again — everything is livened by a cachinnating chorus of the distorted, hilarious elder gods.

The green, bat-winged mocking imps of Tartarus share that chorus with them; nothing else is told of the elder gods outside this delirious interval.

sources.quote: There is a sense of spectral whirling through liquid gulfs of infinity, of dizzying rides through reeling universes on a comet's tail, and of hysterical plunges from the pit to the moon and from the moon back again to the pit, all livened by a cachinnating chorus of the distorted, hilarious elder gods and the green, bat-winged mocking imps of Tartarus.
sources.context: Chapter 3, The Madness from the Sea

facts: A cachinnating chorus of the distorted, hilarious elder gods livens the dream-sense of whirling through liquid gulfs that follows the storm of April 2nd.

needsReview.reason: Figures of the dream-delirium after the storm of April 2nd; possibly a rhetorical flourish rather than distinct beings, and possibly the Great Old Ones under another epithet — no record links them to anything else.
